name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci

      - name: Lint
        run: npm run lint

      - name: Typecheck
        run: npm run typecheck

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

  e2e:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with: { node-version-file: ".nvmrc", cache: npm }
      - run: npm ci
      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
      - name: Build
        run: npm run build
      - name: Smoke test
        run: npm run test:e2e

1	# v2 Deployment Runbook
2	
3	This runbook is the source of truth for deploying the v2 site from a clean
4	checkout. Follow it top-to-bottom for first-time setup, then refer to the
5	later sections for ongoing maintenance.
6	
7	## Prerequisites
8	
9	- **Vercel account** with the CLI installed (`npm i -g vercel`).
10	- **GitHub repository** connected to a Vercel project (the project's
11	  Production branch should track `main`).
12	- **Anthropic Console account** with an API key
13	  (`https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys`). v2 uses
14	  `claude-opus-4-7` by default.
15	- **Embedding provider account** — pick one:
16	  - **Voyage AI** (default, recommended): API key from
17	    `https://dash.voyageai.com/api-keys`, used with `EMBEDDER=voyage`.
18	  - **OpenAI** (alternative): API key from
19	    `https://platform.openai.com/api-keys`, used with `EMBEDDER=openai`.
20	- **Local toolchain**: Node 20+, npm 10+, and `psql` (only needed for the
21	  optional corpus sanity check below).
22	
23	## First-time setup
24	
25	1. **Provision Postgres.** In the Vercel dashboard go to
26	   *Storage → Create Database → Postgres*, accept the defaults, and link
27	   the new database to the Vercel project. This auto-populates
28	   `POSTGRES_URL` and `POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING` for every deployment
29	   environment.
30	
31	2. **Provision KV (Upstash Redis).** In the Vercel dashboard go to
32	   *Storage → Create Database → KV*, link it to the project. This
33	   auto-populates `KV_REST_API_URL` and `KV_REST_API_TOKEN` (plus the
34	   read-only equivalents).
35	
36	3. **Add application environment variables.** In *Project Settings →
37	   Environment Variables* add:
38	
39	   | Variable | Required | Notes |
40	   |---|---|---|
41	   | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | yes | Production + Preview + Development |
42	   | `VOYAGE_API_KEY` | yes (if `EMBEDDER=voyage`) | Production + Preview + Development |
43	   | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | yes (if `EMBEDDER=openai`) | Production + Preview + Development |
44	   | `EMBEDDER` | yes | `voyage` (default) or `openai` |
45	   | `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` | yes | Set to the production URL (e.g. `https://www.michaelquintin.com`); use the Vercel preview URL for Preview scope |
46	   | `LLM_MODEL` | optional | Defaults to `claude-opus-4-7` |
47	   | `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_HOUR` | optional | Defaults from `lib/env.ts` |
48	   | `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_DAY` | optional | Defaults from `lib/env.ts` |
49	
50	   Pick the appropriate scope (Production, Preview, Development) for each
51	   variable. Secrets (`*_API_KEY`) belong in all three; `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL`
52	   is per-environment.
53	
54	4. **Pull env locally.** From the project root:
55	
56	   ```bash
57	   vercel link
58	   vercel env pull .env.local
59	   ```
60	
61	   This writes the linked Postgres, KV, and application variables into
62	   `.env.local` so the local dev server and CLI scripts can connect.
63	
64	5. **Run database migrations.**
65	
66	   ```bash
67	   npm run db:migrate
68	   ```
69	
70	   Migrations are forward-only by default. Re-running is safe.
71	
72	6. **Seed the corpus.**
73	
74	   ```bash
75	   # 1. Drop initial files (.md / .pdf / .txt) into corpus/
76	   # 2. Edit corpus/manifest.json to register each file with id, path, tags
77	   # 3. Ingest:
78	   npm run ingest
79	   ```
80	
81	   The ingest script chunks each file, embeds via the configured
82	   `EMBEDDER`, and writes rows to the `chunks` table.
83	
84	7. **First deploy.** Either:
85	
86	   ```bash
87	   vercel --prod
88	   ```
89	
90	   or push to `main` and let the GitHub integration auto-deploy. Both
91	   paths run the build, the postbuild sitemap step, and the PDF resume
92	   build.
93	
94	## Subsequent deploys
95	
96	- Merge to `main` → Vercel auto-builds and deploys to Production.
97	- Pull requests get Preview deployments automatically; they share the
98	  Production Postgres and KV unless overridden in the env scope.
99	- The sitemap is regenerated by the `postbuild` script on every build.
100	- The resume PDF is regenerated by the `build-pdf` script (see 16/P08).
101	
102	## Updating the corpus
103	
104	1. Add or edit files under `corpus/`.
105	2. Update `corpus/manifest.json` with new entries (or remove stale ones).
106	3. Re-ingest:
107	
108	   ```bash
109	   npm run ingest
110	   ```
111	
112	   Pass `--rebuild` if you want to wipe and reload all chunks rather than
113	   incrementally updating.
114	
115	4. Confirm chunks landed in Postgres:
116	
117	   ```bash
118	   psql $POSTGRES_URL -c "select count(*) from chunks;"
119	   ```
120	
121	5. (Optional) Spot-check voice and groundedness:
122	
123	   ```bash
124	   npm run evals:llm
125	   ```
126	
127	## Cost expectations
128	
129	- **Anthropic API.** v2 ships with `claude-opus-4-7`, which is the
130	  highest-tier model. A typical chat message ranges roughly
131	  **\$0.02–0.10** depending on context size and answer length. The
132	  per-IP and per-day rate limits configured via `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_HOUR`
133	  and `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_DAY` keep the daily ceiling bounded — the worst
134	  case is roughly `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_DAY × max-cost-per-message`.
135	- **Voyage embeddings.** ~\$0.0001 per 1K tokens. A v2-scale corpus
136	  (~500K tokens) costs **<\$0.10** to ingest from scratch. Re-ingest
137	  cost scales linearly with token churn.
138	- **Vercel Postgres.** The free tier comfortably fits the v2 corpus and
139	  expected query volume.
140	- **Vercel KV.** The free tier comfortably fits v2 traffic levels and
141	  the rate-limit counters.
142	- **Total ongoing.** Bounded by the rate limits above.
143	  **~\$5–20/month** is the expected band at low traffic; spikes are
144	  capped by the per-day LLM limit.
145	
146	## Rollback
147	
148	- **Code/runtime rollback.** Vercel keeps every prior deployment. From
149	  the project's *Deployments* tab, find the last known-good deployment
150	  and click *Promote to Production*. No CLI or git revert required.
151	- **Schema rollback.** DB migrations are forward-only by default. To
152	  unwind a schema change, write and apply a corresponding down migration
153	  manually against `$POSTGRES_URL`. There is no auto-down path.
154	- **Corpus rollback.** Corpus is in-repo; revert the offending commit
155	  and re-run `npm run ingest --rebuild`.
156	
157	## Troubleshooting
158	
159	- **"Unauthorized" on chat** → `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is missing or wrong
160	  for the deployment's environment scope. Re-check Vercel env vars and
161	  redeploy.
162	- **"No relevant material" on every query** → corpus is empty or
163	  ingestion silently failed. Re-run `npm run ingest --rebuild` and
164	  verify with the `psql` count above.
165	- **429s spiking** → confirm KV is connected (the rate limiter falls
166	  open if KV is unreachable, so spikes there usually mean it *is*
167	  connected and traffic is genuinely bursty). Raise
168	  `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_HOUR` / `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_DAY` cautiously — every
169	  increase raises the daily cost ceiling.
170	- **PDF resume missing** → the `build-pdf` script failed during deploy.
171	  Check the Vercel build logs for Puppeteer/Playwright errors (missing
172	  Chromium, sandbox flags, font loading). Re-run locally with
173	  `npm run build-pdf` to reproduce.
174

# v2 Deployment Runbook

This runbook is the source of truth for deploying the v2 site from a clean
checkout. Follow it top-to-bottom for first-time setup, then refer to the
later sections for ongoing maintenance.

## Prerequisites

- **Vercel account** with the CLI installed (`npm i -g vercel`).
- **GitHub repository** connected to a Vercel project (the project's
  Production branch should track `main`).
- **Anthropic Console account** with an API key
  (`https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys`). v2 uses
  `claude-opus-4-7` by default.
- **Embedding provider account** — pick one:
  - **Voyage AI** (default, recommended): API key from
    `https://dash.voyageai.com/api-keys`, used with `EMBEDDER=voyage`.
  - **OpenAI** (alternative): API key from
    `https://platform.openai.com/api-keys`, used with `EMBEDDER=openai`.
- **Local toolchain**: Node 20+, npm 10+, and `psql` (only needed for the
  optional corpus sanity check below).

## First-time setup

1. **Provision Postgres.** In the Vercel dashboard go to
   *Storage → Create Database → Postgres*, accept the defaults, and link
   the new database to the Vercel project. This auto-populates
   `POSTGRES_URL` and `POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING` for every deployment
   environment.

2. **Provision KV (Upstash Redis).** In the Vercel dashboard go to
   *Storage → Create Database → KV*, link it to the project. This
   auto-populates `KV_REST_API_URL` and `KV_REST_API_TOKEN` (plus the
   read-only equivalents).

3. **Add application environment variables.** In *Project Settings →
   Environment Variables* add:

   | Variable | Required | Notes |
   |---|---|---|
   | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | yes | Production + Preview + Development |
   | `VOYAGE_API_KEY` | yes (if `EMBEDDER=voyage`) | Production + Preview + Development |
   | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | yes (if `EMBEDDER=openai`) | Production + Preview + Development |
   | `EMBEDDER` | yes | `voyage` (default) or `openai` |
   | `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` | yes | Set to the production URL (e.g. `https://www.michaelquintin.com`); use the Vercel preview URL for Preview scope |
   | `LLM_MODEL` | optional | Defaults to `claude-opus-4-7` |
   | `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_HOUR` | optional | Defaults from `lib/env.ts` |
   | `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_DAY` | optional | Defaults from `lib/env.ts` |

   Pick the appropriate scope (Production, Preview, Development) for each
   variable. Secrets (`*_API_KEY`) belong in all three; `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL`
   is per-environment.

4. **Pull env locally.** From the project root:

   ```bash
   vercel link
   vercel env pull .env.local
   ```

   This writes the linked Postgres, KV, and application variables into
   `.env.local` so the local dev server and CLI scripts can connect.

5. **Run database migrations.**

   ```bash
   npm run db:migrate
   ```

   Migrations are forward-only by default. Re-running is safe.

6. **Seed the corpus.**

   ```bash
   # 1. Drop initial files (.md / .pdf / .txt) into corpus/
   # 2. Edit corpus/manifest.json to register each file with id, path, tags
   # 3. Ingest:
   npm run ingest
   ```

   The ingest script chunks each file, embeds via the configured
   `EMBEDDER`, and writes rows to the `chunks` table.

7. **First deploy.** Either:

   ```bash
   vercel --prod
   ```

   or push to `main` and let the GitHub integration auto-deploy. Both
   paths run the build, the postbuild sitemap step, and the PDF resume
   build.

## Subsequent deploys

- Merge to `main` → Vercel auto-builds and deploys to Production.
- Pull requests get Preview deployments automatically; they share the
  Production Postgres and KV unless overridden in the env scope.
- The sitemap is regenerated by the `postbuild` script on every build.
- The resume PDF is regenerated by the `build-pdf` script (see 16/P08).

## Updating the corpus

1. Add or edit files under `corpus/`.
2. Update `corpus/manifest.json` with new entries (or remove stale ones).
3. Re-ingest:

   ```bash
   npm run ingest
   ```

   Pass `--rebuild` if you want to wipe and reload all chunks rather than
   incrementally updating.

4. Confirm chunks landed in Postgres:

   ```bash
   psql $POSTGRES_URL -c "select count(*) from chunks;"
   ```

5. (Optional) Spot-check voice and groundedness:

   ```bash
   npm run evals:llm
   ```

## Cost expectations

- **Anthropic API.** v2 ships with `claude-opus-4-7`, which is the
  highest-tier model. A typical chat message ranges roughly
  **\$0.02–0.10** depending on context size and answer length. The
  per-IP and per-day rate limits configured via `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_HOUR`
  and `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_DAY` keep the daily ceiling bounded — the worst
  case is roughly `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_DAY × max-cost-per-message`.
- **Voyage embeddings.** ~\$0.0001 per 1K tokens. A v2-scale corpus
  (~500K tokens) costs **<\$0.10** to ingest from scratch. Re-ingest
  cost scales linearly with token churn.
- **Vercel Postgres.** The free tier comfortably fits the v2 corpus and
  expected query volume.
- **Vercel KV.** The free tier comfortably fits v2 traffic levels and
  the rate-limit counters.
- **Total ongoing.** Bounded by the rate limits above.
  **~\$5–20/month** is the expected band at low traffic; spikes are
  capped by the per-day LLM limit.

## Rollback

- **Code/runtime rollback.** Vercel keeps every prior deployment. From
  the project's *Deployments* tab, find the last known-good deployment
  and click *Promote to Production*. No CLI or git revert required.
- **Schema rollback.** DB migrations are forward-only by default. To
  unwind a schema change, write and apply a corresponding down migration
  manually against `$POSTGRES_URL`. There is no auto-down path.
- **Corpus rollback.** Corpus is in-repo; revert the offending commit
  and re-run `npm run ingest --rebuild`.

## Troubleshooting

- **"Unauthorized" on chat** → `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is missing or wrong
  for the deployment's environment scope. Re-check Vercel env vars and
  redeploy.
- **"No relevant material" on every query** → corpus is empty or
  ingestion silently failed. Re-run `npm run ingest --rebuild` and
  verify with the `psql` count above.
- **429s spiking** → confirm KV is connected (the rate limiter falls
  open if KV is unreachable, so spikes there usually mean it *is*
  connected and traffic is genuinely bursty). Raise
  `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_HOUR` / `LLM_RATE_LIMIT_DAY` cautiously — every
  increase raises the daily cost ceiling.
- **PDF resume missing** → the `build-pdf` script failed during deploy.
  Check the Vercel build logs for Puppeteer/Playwright errors (missing
  Chromium, sandbox flags, font loading). Re-run locally with
  `npm run build-pdf` to reproduce.

name: Deployed Smoke

# suite 22/P03 — runs the deployed-environment smoke suite whenever a
# Vercel (or any GitHub-integrated) production deployment reports
# success. The job is also runnable manually so the site can be probed
# on demand.

on:
  deployment_status:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      url:
        description: "Target URL (overrides deployment_status payload)"
        required: false
        type: string

jobs:
  smoke:
    # Only fire for successful Production deployments. workflow_dispatch
    # always runs.
    if: >-
      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||
      (github.event.deployment_status.state == 'success' &&
       github.event.deployment.environment == 'Production')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci

      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium

      - name: Resolve target URL
        id: target
        run: |
          if [ -n "${{ inputs.url }}" ]; then
            echo "url=${{ inputs.url }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "url=${{ github.event.deployment_status.target_url }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Run smoke
        env:
          SMOKE_URL: ${{ steps.target.outputs.url }}
        run: npm run smoke

      - name: Upload smoke results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: smoke-results
          path: |
            smoke-results.json
            playwright-report/
            test-results/
          retention-days: 7

## Smoke alerts

A read-only smoke suite (`e2e/smoke-deployed.spec.ts`) runs against the
production URL on every successful deploy via the GitHub Action at
`.github/workflows/smoke.yml`. Locally, run it against any environment with:

```bash
SMOKE_URL=https://www.michaelquintin.com npm run smoke
# or
npm run smoke -- https://www.michaelquintin.com
```

The runner prints a JSON summary on stdout and exits non-zero on any
failure. Playwright's JSON report is written to `smoke-results.json`
and uploaded as a workflow artifact.

**Hooking up alerts (out of scope for the initial implementation):**

- *Slack.* Add a `notify-slack` step to `.github/workflows/smoke.yml`
  that runs on `if: failure()` and posts to a webhook stored as the
  `SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL` repo secret. Use the
  [`slackapi/slack-github-action`](https://github.com/slackapi/slack-github-action)
  Action and include the deployment URL plus a link to the failed run.
- *Email.* Either pipe the same failure step through a transactional
  email Action (e.g. `dawidd6/action-send-mail`) using SMTP secrets,
  or rely on GitHub's per-user "failed workflow" notifications under
  *Settings → Notifications → Actions*.
- *On-call.* For pager-grade alerting, point the webhook at PagerDuty
  / Opsgenie instead of Slack and keep the Action step otherwise
  identical.

Whichever channel is wired up, it should fire on `if: failure()` only,
and include `${{ github.event.deployment_status.target_url }}` plus a
link to the Action run so the responder can jump straight to the
failing test trace.

# v2 Deployment Runbook

This runbook is the source of truth for deploying the v2 site from a clean
checkout. Follow it top-to-bottom for first-time setup, then refer to the
later sections for ongoing maintenance.

## Prerequisites

- **Vercel account** with the CLI installed (`npm i -g vercel`).
- **GitHub repository** connected to a Vercel project (the project's
  Production branch should track `main`).
- **Anthropic Console account** with an API key
  (`https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys`). v2 uses
  `claude-opus-4-7` by default.
- **Embedding provider account** — pick one:
  - **Voyage AI** (default, recommended): API key from
    `https://dash.voyageai.com/api-keys`, used with `EMBEDDER=voyage`.
  - **OpenAI** (alternative): API key from
    `https://platform.openai.com/api-keys`, used with `EMBEDDER=openai`.

  optional corpus sanity check below).

1. **Provision Postgres.** In the Vercel dashboard go to
   *Storage → Create Database → Postgres*, accept the defaults, and link
   the new database to the Vercel project. This auto-populates
   `POSTGRES_URL` and `POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING` for every deployment
   environment.

2. **Provision KV (Upstash Redis).** In the Vercel dashboard go to
   *Storage → Create Database → KV*, link it to the project. This
   auto-populates `KV_REST_API_URL` and `KV_REST_API_TOKEN` (plus the
   read-only equivalents).

# =============================================================================
# .env.example — single source of truth for environment variables.
#
# Copy to `.env.local` and fill in real values. `.env.local` is gitignored.
# Server-only variables MUST NOT be prefixed with NEXT_PUBLIC_, otherwise
# Next.js will inline them into the client bundle. See docs/v2_env.md for
# how to obtain each value and how to rotate a leaked secret.
# =============================================================================

# === Public site config ===
NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL=http://localhost:3000

# === LLM (Anthropic) ===
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

# === Embeddings ===
VOYAGE_API_KEY=pa-...
EMBEDDER=voyage      # or "openai"
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... # only if EMBEDDER=openai

# === Vector store (Vercel Postgres) ===
POSTGRES_URL=postgres://...
POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING=postgres://...

# === Rate limit (Vercel KV / Upstash) ===
KV_REST_API_URL=https://...
KV_REST_API_TOKEN=...

# === LLM tuning ===
LLM_MODEL=claude-opus-4-7
LLM_RATE_LIMIT_HOUR=20
LLM_RATE_LIMIT_DAY=100
